June 22, 1990
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramentor CA 95804
To Whom it May Concern:
I am writing to request information on behalf of Board of
Equalization Member PauI Carpenter.
Mr. Carpenter is Treasurer of a federal Campaign Committee,
"Friends of Senator Paul Carpenter. n At present, that committee
has assets amounting to approximately $472.00
Ittr. Carpenter wishes to terminate the federal committee, and I
am writing to request your advice regarding whether or not Mr.
Carpenter may deposit the above referenced "federal fundsn into
his Board of Equalization re-election committee account, the
Carpenter Campaigrr Committee.
I would appreciate a reply at your earliest convenience.
Sincerely r
Deputy to Paul CarPenter
t+zr'

sections.question: Whether Mr. Carpenter may deposit federal campaign committee funds (approximately $472.00 from "Friends of Senator Paul Carpenter") into his Board of Equalization re-election committee account, the Carpenter Campaign Committee, upon termination of the federal committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a Board of Equalization member transfer approximately $472 in assets from a terminated federal campaign committee to his state re-election committee account?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or short answer section present. Question reconstructed from body text as no formal QUESTION section header exists.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Mr. Carpenter may deposit federal campaign committee funds (approximately $472.00 from "Friends of Senator Paul Carpenter") into his Board of Equalization re-election committee account, the Carpenter Campaign Committee, upon termination of the federal committee.
